findings.citation: internal/cmd/witness_liveness.go:413 (gt witness replay --apply sh -c on handoff JSON fields)
findings.explanation: Command injection vector: --apply executes sh -c on a command string built from handoff JSON fields (polecat, assigned_agent, etc.). A handoff written or modified by any non-supervisor code path can inject arbitrary shell. Use argv-style exec (no sh -c), and validate handoff field shapes against a strict schema before --apply.

findings.citation: internal/witness/liveness.go RestartWitness synthesizes handoff with StoppedLanes: nil
findings.explanation: The supervised restart path synthesizes a handoff with only heartbeat metadata and an empty StoppedLanes slice, so it does NOT capture stopped lanes, dirty/ahead state, queued scheduler beads, or in-flight cleanup as the spec requires. A restarted Witness will resume without those obligations and may double-recover or skip recovery entirely. RestartWitness must query the witness/manager state at restart time and write a fully-populated handoff.

findings.citation: internal/witness/liveness.go ShouldRestart on missing heartbeat + no production heartbeat writer
findings.explanation: ShouldRestart suppresses restart when no heartbeat exists, but the new witness heartbeat writer (Touch/WriteHeartbeat) has no production caller. Real stalled witnesses will not produce the file the daemon polls, so ShouldRestart always returns false in production for the canonical failure mode this commit is supposed to handle. Wire Touch into the patrol cycle before shipping.

findings.citation: internal/witness/liveness.go ShouldRestart ignores CommandDurationMs and MaxCommandDuration
findings.explanation: The daemon supervisor reads Saturation/IsVeryStale but does not honour CommandDurationMs (per-cycle duration) and MaxCommandDuration thresholds. A saturated witness wedged in a long-running command with a fresh heartbeat will not be restarted, defeating the stalled-session requirement. Honour the documented thresholds.

DefaultHeartbeatStaleThreshold=5m and DefaultHeartbeatVeryStaleThreshold=20m. These defaults are sensible but the watchdog schedule (patrol cycle interval) is not co-located; if the patrol cycle is longer than the very-stale threshold, the watchdog never observes a 'very stale and saturated' state and self-recovery is dead code in practice.

summary: FAIL — durable handoff + heartbeat supervisor is well-structured and has 36 unit tests for the unit-level machinery, but the production path is not safely executable: (a) command-injection in gt witness replay --apply, (b) restart synthesises a handoff missing stopped lanes / dirty state / queued scheduler beads / in-flight cleanup, (c) the new heartbeat writer has no production caller so ShouldRestart is effectively dead code, (d) CommandDurationMs/MaxCommandDuration are ignored. Blocking until all four are fixed.

findings.citation: scripts/cutover-pinned-1.2.0.sh (rollback backup path is relative, not absolute ~/.local/bin/...)
findings.explanation: The rollback backup path is the relative string 'gt.before-pinned-1.2.0-cutover' (saved to the current working directory) rather than the documented absolute ~/.local/bin/.../gt.before-pinned-1.2.0-cutover. The cutover script therefore does not create the documented backup location; the rollback command documented in /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gastown/docs/cutover-pinned-1.2.0.md can fail outside the original working directory. Fix: anchor the backup path under the resolved gt binary directory (the same path used by install/doctor for gt wrapper preservation in 573af6bb).
A regression test that the rollback-backup still triggers on the conditions it was added for would harden the cutover script. Today the rollback-backup is added but never exercised in CI.

summary: FAIL — the throttle rollup fix itself (EvaluateReworkDeferred snapshots before reset, formatReworkDeferredNotification extracted, regression tests proven via temporary revert, RollupSuppressedCount surfaced in dry-run + CLI) is sound and matches gastown-3ip's recommendation. But the union-merged cutover-pinned-1.2.0.sh uses a relative backup path that breaks the documented rollback command. Blocking until the rollback path is anchored under the gt binary directory.

PASS — focused regression tests for convoy-stage JSON output. TestJSONOutput_NoHumanReadableText now fails on runConvoyStage error (was silently ignored). TestReadConvoyIssues_EmptyOutputTreatedAsNoIssues and TestReadConvoyIssues_NonJSONTextTreatedAsNoIssues cover the empty / non-JSON bd list --json cases. No production code change; coverage and error-surfacing both improve.

findings.explanation: Record renders multi-line alert descriptions and passes them through Beads Create/Update, whose real CLI path uses --description values that bd rejects for embedded newlines (the bd create/update commands refuse values with literal newlines unless passed via --body-file). The aggregator will fail when writing the canonical tracking bead. Fix: write the description to a temp file and pass --body-file, or render a single-line summary.

The changed patrol-scan aggregation path is tested only with an in-memory fake BeadsClient, so it misses the real beads CLI/database behaviour required by the production path. Add an integration test that exercises the real Beads.Create/Update with a multi-line description to surface the newline-rejection bug above.

CanonicalSubjectPrefix only maps POLECAT_DIED and ZOMBIE_DETECTED. Adding a third alert class requires changes in three places: keys.go prefix map, ParseSubject, and the canonical tracking bead label format. Consider a registration API instead of a static map.

findings.citation: internal/alerts/aggregator.go Evidence.Timestamp is documented but ignored
findings.explanation: Evidence.Timestamp is documented in the struct but ignored; first_seen and last_seen use the aggregator clock instead of the occurrence timestamp. Either remove the field from the public schema or wire it into the aggregation.

summary: FAIL — the canonical alert keys (RootCauseKey) and aggregator structure are sound and well-tested at the unit level, but the production path is broken: (a) Record renders multi-line descriptions that the real bd CLI rejects for embedded newlines, and (b) tests use an in-memory fake BeadsClient that hides this. Blocking until the description is written to a temp file (--body-file) and an integration test exercises the real CLI.

findings.citation: internal/cmd/sling_content_guard.go:21 (broad 'gt patrol' substring in deacon indicators)
findings.explanation: The deacon roleContentIndicators include the bare substring 'gt patrol'. Any polecat code task whose body mentions 'gt patrol scan', 'gt patrol report', or even 'fix gt patrol timeout' will be flagged as Deacon content and refused from mol-polecat-work. The indicator should be more specific ('deacon patrol', '--agent-bead hq-deacon', 'deacon session') — already present, but 'gt patrol' is too broad and overlaps with the very CLI polecats are dispatched to maintain. Tighten the substring or weight it lower.

findings.citation: internal/cmd/sling_content_guard_test.go (no false-positive case for polecat code task that mentions patrol CLI)
findings.explanation: Tests cover the helper but do not cover schedule/dispatch call paths or a patrol-CLI code-task false-positive case. Add TestValidateBeadContentForFormula_PolecatPatrolTaskMentioningPatrol which asserts the broad substring does NOT trigger the guard.

formulaTargetRole currently only contains 'mol-polecat-work: polecat'. Other formulas (e.g. mol-deacon-work, mol-mayor-work, mol-witness-work) are not yet registered; the guard silently passes for them. Add entries as each formula lands, or skip the check entirely when targetFormula is not in the map (with a clear log line).

summary: FAIL — sling_content_guard.go adds ValidateBeadContentForFormula and detectConflictingRole, and capacity_dispatch.go + sling_schedule.go wire the new guard. However the deacon roleContentIndicators include the bare substring 'gt patrol', which would cause the guard to refuse legitimate polecat code tasks that mention gt patrol (e.g. 'fix gt patrol scan timeout'). Tighten the substring or anchor it more strictly before shipping.

findings.explanation: The inventory classifies each ad-hoc store as retain/quarantine/archive/legacy but does not list concrete migration tickets or owners for the quarantine/archive actions (mayor/gastown/.beads hq-* duplicates, gastown-orphan-beadstore, bdglobal/beads_global). Without follow-up beads this becomes a doc-only audit, not a remediation plan.

summary: PASS — design-only inventory. Constraint that no raw Dolt edits occur is explicit. Same constraint is honored by the host rules (do not edit .dolt/, nom s/, or LOCK files). Recommend filing concrete follow-up beads for each non-canonical store but this commit itself is sound.

findings.explanation: Tests call the underlying runMayorDecisionRecord/runMayorDecisionList functions directly rather than the cobra command tree. A wiring regression in cmd/mayor_decision.go would not be caught (e.g. flag binding breakage, RunE not invoked, or persistent-flag-state bleed between invocations).

HandleMergeFailed tests cover defer/hold/park/resume but not the cross-rig related_bead path. The hq-12zq regression replay is in scope, but a test where the Mayor decision lives in a different rig from the source bead would harden the cross-rig routing claim.

summary: PASS — Mayor decision precedence guards + gt mayor decision CLI test coverage. Active DEFER/HOLD/PARK on a source bead now prevents automated rework spawn (resetAbandonedBead) and rework nudge (HandleMergeFailed). hq-12zq regression replayed. resume does not hold. Test depth adequate but call funcs not the cobra tree (known gap per shard C).
